jobTitle: Scientific Employee (f/m/d) Head of the XRCT laboratory at the Helmholtz Institute Freiberg for Resource Technology
jobDescription: With cutting-edge research in the fields of ENERGY, HEALTH and MATTER, around 1,500 employees from more than 70 nations at Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR) are committed to mastering the great challenges facing society today. At the Helmholtz Institute Freiberg for Resource Technology (HIF), innovative technologies for a sustainable Circular Economy for minerals and metalliferous resources are developed to provide and use mineral and metal-bearing raw materials more efficiently and to recycle them in an environmentally friendly way. The Helmholtz Institute Freiberg for Resource Technology (HIF) is looking for a Scientific Employee (f/m/d) Head of the XRCT laboratory.
jobTasks: Management of the X-Ray Computed Tomography Laboratory at HIF
Supervision and operation of the X-Ray CT scanner CoreTom equipped with a spectral detector
Supervision and implementation of image processing
Development of the automated and standardized workflows established at the HIF
Supervision and guidance of students, employees and guest scientists
Close interdisciplinary cooperation with the other laboratories in the analytical department and with all other departments at the HIF
Publish in peer-reviewed journals and present research results at conferences
Preparation of funding applications for the technical and instrumental expansion and the further development of methods and software packages
